name: Japanese Onmyodo
description: Onmyodo is a traditional Japanese esoteric cosmology, a system of natural science, astronomy, divination, and magic. It originated in China, based on the philosophies of Wu Xing (the Five Elements) and Yin and Yang. Introduced to Japan in the 6th century, it was integrated with native Shinto beliefs and practices, eventually becoming a distinct system. Onmyodo practitioners, known as Onmyoji, were skilled in divination, exorcism, geomancy, and calendar-making. They served the imperial court and later, the shogunate, advising on matters of state and personal fortune. Their powers were believed to influence natural phenomena and ward off evil spirits. Key aspects include the manipulation of shikigami (familiar spirits), the use of Ofuda (talismans), and the performance of rituals to maintain balance and harmony. While its official influence waned after the Meiji Restoration, Onmyodo continues to influence Japanese culture through folklore, literature, and popular media. The practice emphasizes understanding and harmonizing with the natural world to achieve desired outcomes and maintain spiritual well-being. Onmyodo aims to control and manipulate the flow of ki, or life energy, to influence events and protect individuals from harm, blending Chinese cosmology with indigenous Japanese spiritual traditions.
subtitle: Esoteric Cosmology and Spiritual Practice

keyMyths.title: Abe no Seimei's Origins
keyMyths.description: Abe no Seimei, a legendary Onmyoji, is often depicted as having supernatural parentage, with a Kitsune (fox spirit) mother. This origin story highlights his innate connection to the spiritual realm and his exceptional magical abilities.
